faqs.question: How do I know if I have carpenter ants in my Eltingville home?
faqs.answer: Signs of carpenter ants include piles of wood shavings (frass) near baseboards or windowsills, rustling sounds inside walls, and sightings of large black ants, especially in spring. You may also notice winged ants emerging indoors during warm months, indicating an established colony within your structure.

faqs.question: What does carpenter ant control cost in Eltingville?
faqs.answer: Carpenter ant control in Eltingville typically ranges from $350 to $2,000 depending on the severity of the infestation, the size of your property, and the number of treatments required. We provide free inspections and detailed quotes before beginning any work.

faqs.question: How long does carpenter ant treatment take to work?
faqs.answer: You'll typically see a significant reduction in carpenter ant activity within 1-2 weeks after treatment. Complete colony elimination may take 4-6 weeks as worker ants carry treatment back to the nest. We offer follow-up visits to ensure the entire colony is eradicated and provide ongoing prevention.

faqs.question: Can carpenter ants cause serious damage to my Eltingville home?
faqs.answer: Yes, carpenter ants can cause significant structural damage over time by excavating wood to build their nests. They prefer damp or damaged wood but can infest sound wood as colonies expand. Early detection and professional treatment are crucial to prevent costly repairs to joists, beams, and framing.
